title: BNCI 2015-008 Center Speller P300 dataset
description: A P300-based brain-computer interface dataset comprising EEG recordings from 13 healthy participants performing a gaze-independent speller task using covert spatial and feature attention. The dataset includes data from three speller variants (Hex-o-Spell, Cake Speller, and Center Speller) with 63-channel recordings at 250 Hz, preprocessed and annotated for event-related potential analysis. This benchmark dataset demonstrates high classification accuracy (88-97%) and is designed for evaluating BCI speller performance and communication applications.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a 63-channel Brain Products actiCAP system at 250 Hz sampling rate with a 10-10 montage, referenced to the left mastoid. Online bandpass filtering (0.016-250 Hz) was applied during acquisition. Offline preprocessing included downsampling to 250 Hz, lowpass filtering below 49 Hz using a Chebyshev filter, baseline correction using a -200 ms prestimulus interval, and re-referencing to linked mastoids. Participants performed a visual speller task requiring covert attention to target symbols while maintaining central fixation, with stimulus onset asynchrony of 200 ms.
